content_en:
The chapter on salah (prayer) is the longest section of Al-Mulakhas al-Fiqhi, reflecting the centrality of the five daily prayers in Islamic practice. Sheikh al-Fawzan begins by citing the evidence for the obligation of salah from the Quran, the Sunnah, and scholarly consensus. He notes that salah is the first deed for which a Muslim will be held accountable on the Day of Judgment, and that its abandonment is a matter of grave consequence.

The chapter covers the conditions that must be met before salah becomes obligatory on an individual: Islam, sanity, and reaching the age of puberty. It then addresses the prerequisites that must be fulfilled before the prayer itself is valid — ritual purity (tahara), covering the awrah (private parts), facing the qibla, the entry of the prayer time, and the intention (niyyah). Sheikh al-Fawzan discusses the prayer times in detail, explaining the beginning and end of each of the five prayers with reference to celestial indicators and the relevant hadith.

The obligatory pillars (arkan) of salah are enumerated and explained: the opening takbir (saying Allahu Akbar), standing for those able to do so, recitation of al-Fatiha, the ruku' (bowing), rising from ruku', the two prostrations (sujud) with the interval of sitting between them, the final tashahhud, and the taslim (closing salutation). Sheikh al-Fawzan explains what is obligatory (wajib), what is a Sunnah, and what are recommended acts (mustahabbat) within the prayer, enabling the reader to distinguish between elements whose omission invalidates the prayer and those that merely reduce its reward.

The chapter also covers congregational prayer (salat al-jama'ah), explaining its ruling (wajib according to the Hanbali school), the virtues established in the hadith, the roles of the imam and the follower, and the rulings for a latecomer who catches part of the prayer. Friday prayer (Jumu'ah), the prayers of the two Eids, the prayer for rain (Istisqa'), and the prayer in times of fear (Khawf) are treated with their specific conditions and procedures, giving readers a comprehensive overview of the entire domain of salah.
content_id:
Bab tentang shalat adalah bagian terpanjang dari Al-Mulakhas al-Fiqhi, mencerminkan sentralitas lima shalat harian dalam praktik Islam. Syekh al-Fawzan memulai dengan mengutip dalil-dalil wajibnya shalat dari Al-Quran dan Sunnah yang mutawatir, kemudian membahas secara terperinci semua aspek praktis ibadah shalat.

Cakupan pembahasan meliputi: waktu-waktu shalat wajib, adzan dan iqamat, syarat-syarat sahnya shalat (suci, menutup aurat, menghadap kiblat, masuk waktu), rukun-rukun shalat, wajibat shalat, sunnah-sunnah shalat, hal-hal yang membatalkan shalat, sujud sahwi, shalat berjamaah dan imamah, shalat Jumat, shalat musafir (qasar dan jamak), shalat dalam keadaan sakit, shalat khauf, shalat sunnah rawatib, tahajjud, witir, dhuha, shalat Id, shalat gerhana, shalat istisqa', dan shalat jenazah.
